metadata.description: Weather-triggered advisory rules for potato farming in Kenya, derived from agronomic literature

sources.title: Climate Resilient Potato Production Handbook - NPCK & CRAFT

rules.name: Late Blight - Infection Trigger
rules.risk: Phytophthora infestans infection triggered. Late blight can destroy entire crop within days if untreated.
actions: Apply Mancozeb fungicide immediately if not sprayed in last 7 days
For susceptible varieties: spray every 7 days from emergence onwards — do NOT wait for symptoms
For resistant varieties (Kenya Faulu, Karibu, Chaguo, Mavuno, Sifa, Tigoni, Asante): spray when symptoms first appear
Alternate Mancozeb with Metalaxyl to prevent resistance

rules.name: Late Blight - Accelerated Spread
rules.risk: Warm + humid conditions accelerate blight spread. Infection can move from first spots to total crop loss rapidly.
actions: Reduce spray interval to every 7 days or less
Scout daily for grey spots on lower leaf tips and edges
Do NOT use sprinkler irrigation — wet canopy promotes blight
Switch to drip or furrow irrigation

rules.name: Late Blight - Spray Window
rules.risk: none — this is an opportunity window
actions: Good window to apply Mancozeb — calm and dry conditions for good coverage
Spray early morning or late evening
Use 6 knapsacks per acre for full coverage

rules.name: Early Blight - Favorable Conditions
rules.risk: Alternaria solani causes brown scorching spots on leaves. Warm humid conditions favour infection.
actions: Spray Mancozeb from emergence, every 7 days
Scout for brown concentric ring spots on older leaves

rules.name: Black Scurf - Cold Wet Soil at Planting
rules.risk: Rhizoctonia solani (black scurf) promoted by cold (<7°C) or waterlogged soil. Causes hard brown masses on tubers, dead tissue on stems.
actions: Do NOT plant today — soil is too cold or too wet
Wait for soil to warm above 7°C and drain
Use certified disease-free seed

rules.name: Bacterial Wilt - Waterlogging Spread Risk
rules.risk: Heavy rain carries Ralstonia solanacearum via surface runoff from infected fields. Bacterial wilt is soil-persistent and devastating.
actions: Check drainage channels are clear — runoff spreads bacterial wilt
Inspect for wilting plants despite adequate moisture (diagnostic sign)
If wilt found: rogue diseased plants immediately, do NOT compost them
Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for >2 years if field infected

rules.name: Blackleg - Wet Field Risk
rules.risk: Pectobacterium (blackleg/soft rot) thrives in wet, flooded fields. Slimy black stem bases, tuber rot.
actions: Check drainage — do not allow standing water around plants
Do NOT hill up while soil is this wet — spreads disease
If harvesting soon, wait for dry weather

rules.name: Common Scab - Dry Soil at Tuber Set
rules.risk: Streptomyces scab promoted by dry soil at tuber initiation. Rough, cracked, scabby skin reduces market value.
actions: Irrigate NOW — soil moisture at tuber set is critical to prevent scab
Maintain consistent soil moisture through tuber initiation period
Avoid over-liming (alkaline soil worsens scab)

rules.name: Heat Stress - Tuberization Decline
rules.risk: Above 21°C daily average causes sharp decline in tuber formation. Above 29°C, little or no tubers form.
actions: Irrigate to reduce soil temperature
Apply mulch to conserve soil moisture and moderate temperature
If temperature consistently >25°C, expect reduced yield

rules.name: Frost Alert
rules.risk: Frost scorching destroys potato foliage. Can cause total crop loss. Primary climate hazard in Nyandarua County.
actions: Apply mulch to protect crop from frost tonight
If frost damage has occurred, assess crop — severely damaged crops may not recover
Plan future planting dates to avoid frost-prone periods

rules.name: Wind - No Spray Conditions
rules.risk: Wind causes spray drift, poor coverage, and wasted chemicals.
actions: Do NOT spray in windy conditions — chemicals will drift
Wait for calm conditions (early morning or late evening)

rules.name: Waterlogging Alert
rules.risk: Waterlogging causes root suffocation, nutrient leaching, fungal diseases (blackleg, black scurf), and crop washout.
actions: Clear all drainage channels and cut-off drains immediately
Do NOT irrigate — soil is saturated
Do NOT hill up — wet soil compaction spreads disease
If planting: delay until soil drains

rules.name: Irrigation Needed - Below Weekly Threshold

crop_context.note: Critical stages: germination, flowering, tuber setting, tuber bulking

rules.risk: Potato needs minimum 25mm/week. Deficit reduces yield and tuber quality.
actions: Start irrigation — rainfall below 25mm threshold this week
Use drip irrigation (recommended) — avoids late blight risk from wet canopy
Avoid sprinkler irrigation — promotes late blight
Prioritise watering at tuber initiation and bulking stages

rules.name: Aphid Surge - Dry Conditions
rules.risk: Drought conditions cause aphid and whitefly population surge. Aphids are vectors for Potato Leaf Roll Virus and Potato Virus Y.
actions: Check yellow water traps (6 per acre) for aphid counts
Spray Alphacypermethrin or Dimethoate (30-40ml per 20L water) if aphids detected
Apply mineral oils to prevent virus transmission
Rogue any plants showing virus symptoms (leaf curling, mosaic)

rules.name: Potato Tuber Moth - Warm Dry Risk
rules.risk: Warm and dry weather favours PTM infestation. Larvae tunnel into tubers in field and storage.
actions: Check ridges — re-earth up if any tubers are exposed
Maintain ridge height at 25cm minimum
Cut foliage at 80 days after planting (prevents larvae on foliage from moving into tubers)
Harvest immediately after dehaulming — don't leave tubers in ground

rules.name: PTM - Exposed Tubers After Rain/Wind
rules.risk: Heavy rain and wind erode ridges, exposing tubers to PTM egg laying and greening.
actions: Inspect ridges after heavy rain/wind — check for exposed tubers
Re-earth up immediately if tubers are visible
Do NOT wait — exposed tubers attract PTM within hours

rules.name: Root-Knot Nematode - Warm Moist Soil

crop_context.note: Optimal at ~27°C. No egg-laying below 14.2°C or above 31.7°C

rules.risk: Meloidogyne spp. root galling peaks at warm soil temperatures. New generation in 25 days under favourable conditions.
actions: If nematodes are a known problem in this field, plant trap crops (Mexican marigold) between seasons
Rotate with non-host crops
Avoid furrow irrigation on sandy soils (promotes nematodes)

rules.name: Cutworm Risk - Dry Spell at Emergence
rules.risk: Cutworms (Agrotis spp.) active during dry spells. Cut emerging shoots at ground level, causing plant death.
actions: Scout for cut stems and foliage holes — cutworms are nocturnal, check early morning
Spray Alphacypermethrin or Dimethoate (30-40ml per 20L) at 10 days after germination
Hill up to cover exposed tubers and stem bases

rules.name: Harvest Window - Dry and Overcast
rules.risk: none — this is the ideal harvest window
actions: Good day to harvest — dry soil with cloud cover is ideal
Confirm skin set: rub thumb on tuber — skin should not slip
Leave tubers on ground max 3 hours for soil to fall off, then move to shade
Do NOT expose tubers to direct sunlight — causes greening

rules.name: No Harvest - Wet Soil
rules.risk: Wet harvest increases bruising, disease spread, and dirty tubers. Blackleg infection rises sharply.
actions: Do NOT harvest today — wait for dry conditions
Wet-harvested tubers rot in storage

rules.name: Harvest Alert - Sun Exposure Risk
rules.risk: Clear skies + warm temperatures cause rapid greening and sunscald of harvested tubers.
actions: If harvesting today, move tubers to shade within 3 hours
Cover harvested tubers during field transport
Prefer harvesting early morning or late afternoon if skies are clear

rules.name: Hilling - Wet Soil Warning
rules.risk: Earthing up on wet soil causes compaction and spreads late blight and blackleg.
actions: Do NOT hill up today — soil is too wet
Wait for soil to dry (at least 2 days without rain)
Hilling on wet soil compacts it and spreads fungal diseases

rules.name: Nitrogen Leaching Risk - Heavy Rain on Young Crop

crop_context.note: 40-50 DAP: crop demand low, heavy rain leaches applied N

rules.risk: Heavy rain at 40-50 DAP leaches nitrogen before crop demand peaks. Wasted fertilizer.
actions: If N not yet applied, delay until rain eases — it will leach
If N was recently applied, consider a supplemental top-dress at tuber bulking
Split N applications across the season to reduce leaching risk

rules.name: Fertilizer Window - Moist Soil
rules.risk: none — this is a fertilizer application opportunity
actions: Good day to apply fertilizer — soil is moist but not waterlogged
Apply DAP at planting; CAN as top-dress
Incorporate urea into soil immediately after spreading

rules.name: Planting Window - Rain Onset
rules.risk: none — this is the optimal planting window
actions: Rains have started and soil is warm enough — plant now
Use well-sprouted seed (3+ sprouts per tuber)
Plant at 30cm spacing, cover to 15cm depth
Planting at exact rain onset gives best yield and escapes late-season disease pressure

rules.name: Do Not Plant - Cold or Waterlogged
rules.risk: Cold soil (<7°C) promotes black scurf. Waterlogged soil promotes blackleg and root rot. Both cause crop failure.
actions: Do NOT plant today — soil is too cold or too wet
Wait for soil to warm and drain before planting

rules.name: Drought Alert - Crop Stress
rules.risk: Prolonged dry spell causes crop failure, reduced tuber quality, and aphid/whitefly population surge.
actions: Irrigate immediately if water available — drip irrigation preferred
Apply mulch to conserve soil moisture
Increase aphid scouting — dry conditions cause pest surge
Check yellow water traps for aphid counts

rules.name: General Spray Window
rules.risk: none — this is an opportunity window
actions: Good conditions for spraying — calm and dry
Apply early morning or late evening for best results
Use protective clothing during application

rules.name: No Spray - Rain or Wind
rules.risk: Rain washes off chemicals. Wind causes drift and poor coverage.
actions: Do NOT spray today — chemicals will wash off or drift
Wait for calm, dry conditions

rules.conflict: Spray needed but rain/wind prevents it
rules.resolution: Weather wins. Delay spray. But do NOT skip — apply at first available window.

rules.conflict: Irrigation needed but soil is waterlogged
rules.resolution: Waterlogging wins. Do not irrigate saturated soil.

rules.conflict: Harvest season but soil is wet
rules.resolution: Wet harvest warning wins. Delay harvest.

rules.conflict: Hilling due but soil is wet
rules.resolution: Wet soil warning wins. Delay hilling.
